hawkish_phrases: risks around the inflation projection balanced in that year. Thereafter, the staff continued to view the risks around the inflation forecast as skewed to the upside
the risk that inflation could prove to be more persistent than expected
Participants emphasized the importance of ensuring that longer-term inflation expectations remained well anchored
some measures of inflation expectations raising the possibility that inflation would prove to be more persistent
dovish_phrases: downside risks to employment and economic activity...had risen
there was a risk that the labor market would weaken in coming months
the staff viewed the possibility that the economy would enter a recession to be almost as likely as the baseline forecast
The unemployment rate was forecast moving above the staff's estimate of its natural rate by the end of this year
summary: The Committee unanimously held rates at 4.25-4.50 percent while acknowledging heightened uncertainty and explicitly stating that risks of both higher unemployment and higher inflation had risen, with balanced directional risk language and no forward guidance.
